summary: Iteration 3 — validated the pip+yarn reversal (uv artifacts removed). Created a clean Python 3.12.13 venv in /tmp and ran `pip install -r /app/api/requirements.txt` — exit 0. All 8 pinned deps install at exact requested versions; all non-stdlib imports in server.py resolve (fastapi/starlette/pydantic/supabase/postgrest/bcrypt/PyJWT/dotenv/email_validator/cryptography). `PYTHONPATH=/app/backend python -c 'from server import app'` yields 49 routes — proves the pip dep set is sufficient for the FastAPI app. No uv artifacts remain (pyproject.toml + uv.lock absent). vercel.json contains `VERCEL_PYTHON_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pip` in build.env and exact buildCommand. `yarn install --frozen-lockfile` + `CI=false yarn build` produced /app/frontend/build/index.html (Compiled successfully, 188KB gzipped JS). Live backend healthy: /api/health = {ok:true, db_reachable:true, db_user_count:10}. Full pytest suite: 47-48/49 pass (identical to iteration 2 net of one flaky admin suspend_user test which passed on retry; the persistent failure is the same known cosmetic 500-vs-404 on invalid-UUID book lookup, pre-existing).

minor.issue: Still returns 500 instead of 404 when book_id is not a valid UUID (Supabase raises APIError before empty-result path). Pre-existing, unrelated to this iteration.
Test intermittently returned 500 on first run, passed on retry — likely Supabase transient / rate. Not a regression from config changes.

backend_import_after_install.command: PYTHONPATH=/app/backend python -c 'from server import app; print(len(app.routes), "routes")'

requirements_completeness_check.coverage: ALL covered — either directly pinned in api/requirements.txt or as a mandatory transitive of fastapi/supabase/pydantic. Verified by successful `from server import app` on the pip-only venv.

no_uv_artifacts_check.buildCommand_exact_match: cd frontend && yarn install && CI=false yarn build

backend_health_check.note: Preview pod runs Python 3.11.15 (unchanged); Vercel will run 3.12 per .python-version

pytest_regression.delta_vs_iteration_2: 0 new persistent failures; matches prior iteration

action_items: SAFE TO REDEPLOY on Vercel: /app/api/requirements.txt (and root /app/requirements.txt) fully cover all runtime imports on Python 3.12; vercel.json correctly forces pip via VERCEL_PYTHON_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pip; buildCommand runs yarn as requested. Push to GitHub and trigger Vercel redeploy — final verification is user-side only.
(Optional, unrelated) Guard GET /api/books/{id} against non-UUID input to convert 500 → 404 — carried from iterations 1 & 2.
critical_code_review_comments: Root /app/requirements.txt and /app/api/requirements.txt are byte-identical (8 lines each) — Vercel will find api/requirements.txt first for the serverless function; the root copy is defense-in-depth. Recommend a small comment or CI check to keep them in sync on future dep bumps.

vercel.json uses build.env.VERCEL_PYTHON_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pip — this is the current supported flag to opt out of uv. If Vercel changes the flag name in a future runtime, the fix will silently regress; add a build log check post-deploy to confirm pip (not uv) was used.

server.py imports `starlette` and `postgrest` directly but neither is listed explicitly in requirements.txt — currently satisfied as transitive deps of fastapi (0.116.1 → starlette 0.47.3) and supabase (2.16.0 → postgrest 1.1.1). If a future fastapi/supabase minor bump drops one of these, the app would import-fail. Consider adding starlette + postgrest as explicit pins with a comment noting they are used directly.

api/index.py mutates sys.path at import time to reach /backend/server.py — works on Vercel because vercel.json's `includeFiles: 'backend/**'` ships the backend folder alongside the function. Confirmed intentional; no change needed.
.python-version = 3.12 (unpinned patch) — Vercel will pick latest 3.12.x runtime, which is desirable for security patches.
test_credentials: admin@bookbridge.in / Admin@123, aditi@demo.in / demo123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md; used by pytest suite)
seed_data_creation: None. Existing pytest suite creates TEST_-prefixed transient data via API.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 reversed the uv-based approach per user request. Clean-venv `pip install -r api/requirements.txt` on Python 3.12.13 exits 0; `from server import app` yields 49 routes on that venv. No pyproject.toml / uv.lock present. vercel.json forces pip via VERCEL_PYTHON_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pip. Frontend `yarn install --frozen-lockfile && CI=false yarn build` produces build/index.html cleanly. Final Vercel deploy verification is user-side (push + redeploy). Only outstanding pre-existing issue: GET /api/books/{invalid-uuid} returns 500 instead of 404.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A this iteration — validation only. Previous iteration's uv fix was reversed per user's explicit request to standardize on pip + yarn. New Vercel config uses the official VERCEL_PYTHON_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pip build-env flag to opt out of uv entirely; requirements.txt covers all direct + indirect imports at pinned versions compatible with Python 3.12.
